name: cable rope lying on floor tricep extension
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine and set it to the lowest position.
Lie on the floor facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Hold the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Keep your upper arms stationary and slowly lower the rope towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Attach a rope to a cable machine and set it to the lowest position. Lie on the floor facing up, with your head towards the cable machine. Hold the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your upper arms stationary and slowly lower the rope towards your forehead, bending your elbows. Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Avoid supine positions after the first trimester due to vena cava compression. Incline or side-lie alternatives preferred.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
